topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: What can users find in the prompts.chat library?
entries.a: Users can find a curated collection of prompt examples for AI chat models, which can be used to enhance interactions with AI assistants.

entries.q: How can someone contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: Individuals can contribute prompts by visiting the prompts.chat website and submitting new prompts, which will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free guide that teaches prompt engineering through over 25 chapters, covering topics from basics to advanced techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the 'Prompting for Kids' section?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' section provides an interactive, game-based adventure aimed at teaching children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through engaging puzzles and stories.

entries.q: How can users self-host their own prompt library?
entries.a: Users can self-host their own prompt library by using commands like 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' to create a new library with custom branding and features.

entries.q: What integrations are available with prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers integrations such as a command-line interface (CLI) and plugins for AI tools, allowing users to easily access and utilize the prompt library.
Prompts.chat has been featured in Forbes, referenced by Harvard and Columbia, and has received over 143k stars on GitHub, highlighting its significance in the AI community.
